textByHash.c35cddda8a9914c2b46c1d7fdd11d3bc4d614a3b: It's a real pleasure to meet you, My Arm. And you too. I look forward tremendously to working with you both.
textByHash.c10e7e765e23509251f415f947f7d339b18fbc8c: You really shouldn't speak to her that way, Mother. It's incredibly rude.
textByHash.d9ebb6264eca6cd50796c2adb4d905989ed28815: She doesn't want that, you know.
textByHash.5a6bf80afcbf83b8ed512d349d679ca002801bbe: In return, we can teach you all about the special fires we've invented. They can be very useful.
textByHash.5ddee1393e2a495f256e179d9eb97ff59c4f3e21: Oh, Mother's been annoying me for years. He doesn't care about anything except fighting. Whatever I invent for him, he ignores it completely if he can't use it in combat.
textByHash.76dc5bd739e099c38cb818d3d6e33cf0301a76d0: That's why he dismissed My Arm's farming expertise too. He's really holding Weiss back. Trolls should aspire to be more than just lumbering monsters.
textByHash.9a4e7ad562d5b2d153cc9bfceac66fac837a3f9e: Mother won't change himself, and none of us are strong enough to force him, especially with Don't Know What guarding him... but maybe My Arm can help.
textByHash.8399628b9d56e5979d8ff1d17de320c535adbb8d: I deplore violence. No, I want Mother to let Snowflake marry My Arm, instead of marrying Don't Know What. Snowflake's smart, and she'll rule us better.
textByHash.363338e0c05ccbfc5ccf5b36263c9a599e9b79a5: But if she marries Don't Know What, he'll insist on ruling Weiss himself like Mother does - and he's no better than Mother. So I'd rather My Arm stayed here and married Snowflake.
textByHash.ff9facdd37a3725455d1e4dfa7faf09261817738: So we will need Mother to gain respect for My Arm, so that he'll consent to the marriage.
textByHash.555a2d64e429fb9ff5ba1bb976e970b8df51a870: Let's not have any unnecessary killing.
textByHash.2de4a0010d81b1d0bda22d54fee346c7b853fa87: Mother's strength saved this tribe, in the past. That's how he became chief. He truly believes that if our chief is not physically strong, we will die out.
textByHash.1e1da86cc6902bca39ef194f56a8882df6e5f048: You see, we have not always lived in Weiss. We used to live further south, near the dwarf town they call Keldagrim, until we were driven out...
textByHash.487ca988e70a8eb1aa401074f3a93950c7270468: That Wise Young Man drove us out into the barren icy mountains. Without Mother's strength to overcome obstacles and catch food, I believe we would have perished.
textByHash.70c1e7a2cc8716bcf6c7c0f64d0bbb2ba93eafcb: Yes, that's right. They treated him as a great hero after he drove us out of our tunnels.
textByHash.11a74b4a601a90fd3073415b37cd68809aadd4c8: Yes! If you kill the human who drove us out from the Keldagrim tunnels, Mother will call you a hero and be pleased for you to marry Snowflake. Can you go and do it?
textByHash.e2c6febcc9e96b3c7a27ecc12780b4aae9e13c7d: Ah, no. He surely won't agree to die. But perhaps he can pretend he's dead, while My Arm tricks Mother into believing it. Can you ask him?
textByHash.a123a52f1e9bd74894762aa9ade8b5552c42c470: Ah. Mother is our chief, and as his daughter, Snowflake is the heir to the throne of Weiss. So Snowflake or her future husband will rule this town. My Arm may be a charming chappy...
textByHash.b38dc9edcdf1849a44951b20b72736e2600e1779: ... but Snowflake shouldn't be expected to run away from Weiss, abandoning what's hers. No, we should try to keep My Arm here.
textByHash.c4ce918b527fe74fc08954ebdfbdccfbdbf32a1c: Yes, that would impress Mother very much.
textByHash.5fe6cd1b1ea4fbd1c1e5e8413935c4310770cabf: My Arm is clearly a great warrior, beyond the skills of any Weiss troll. We should welcome him into the tribe.
textByHash.c40bc6007969bf39268b65e0b8a5e6bc921f9764: Come on, Mother. That man was our mortal enemy, and now My Arm's brought us his corpse. Accept My Arm as a friend and let's move on like civilised people.
textByHash.707e20d38836853c2eedf2e7dfa4dd23a45cf202: Um... Don't worry, Mother, this is totally normal for a dead wizard. It's the magic escaping from the cadaver...
textByHash.de01eb38fc6a017fedee46d7420f60cee86f3d55: Snowflake, are you there? Reason tells me there is light in here, but my mind sees darkness.
textByHash.f32f05461b594379f732f9d1a64851b3dbde04fa: ... no, I don't think so. I am glad you are here with me, Snowflake. I must tell you something...
textByHash.7250663190363a5522a929da479e2c82808953a7: I can smell that Mother has lit a Fire of Domination, up above. He never cared for most of my inventions, but he liked that one.
textByHash.491b49b0e51bd543dec963dc7fc5d85e50164f42: That's the one that traps his enemies, and helps him beat them to a pulp. Definitely his favourite. He must intend to kill you all.
textByHash.a7c5ee3cc17db99b6a1000705082c61c1317f9b3: ... I suppose I have no secrets, now, even from the age- old enemy of Weiss. But you and I are thinkers, and in other circumstances we might have been friends.
textByHash.d244ba5fd54f28bd473480def48fc9257ccf3983: These salts in the mine - I invented ways to combine them, unlock their power. In a fire, they can work wonders.
textByHash.e069f7d43b99d6d9400b76d531ad67f7958daad3: The Fire of Domination will block teleportation magicks, and it also aids Mother in a fight. It makes him incredibly dangerous.
textByHash.d5c4932b28adfdd4ce6cd7861e8d67c9ee4367f1: I wrote down my inventions, but many of my notes are lost. Mother only ever cared about the Fire of Domination, and fires he could use for combat.
textByHash.63ce657b327c21f77e450592187b33a45dec4575: It can be hard to interest trolls in the delicate skill of Firemaking, sometimes. But perhaps Snowflake can find my lost notes, and carry on the science. She's far smarter than her father.
textByHash.ff84dbcbecc7e44d1a4be0e236dd203aa689e630: But I never invented a fire that could stave off the eternal dark, and... I feel it coming.
textByHash.6abfef82d0a65025e1f6954d8e621e4ea91821ef: Snowflake, you must take over Weiss. You saw what Mother did to you, and to me, and to My Arm. Trolls will never be more than thuggish monsters as long as he rules.
